blocks.text: DTC P1841: Driven pulley speed sensor Backward direction detection function failure

The driven pulley speed sensor is mounted on the transmission case. The driven pulley speed sensor contains a semiconductor that detects the driven pulley position. As the driven pulley rotates, the magnetic flux of the driven pulley speed sensor fluctuates. The magnetic flux variation is converted into a pulse signal and sent to the PGM-FI ECU. The direction of rotation of the rotor is detected by the voltage level of the pulse signal. If there is a discrepancy between the vehicle running status and the direction of rotation indicated by the pulse signal, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
Driven Pulley Speed When the sensor rotation direction output signal continues to indicate forward rotation for more than 2 seconds during backward traveling.

items.text: Select lever is turned to "R" position and the vehicle is driven at a speed [vehicle speed] of 3 km/h or more for at least 5 seconds.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the D indicator light will flash without the PGM-FI warning light illuminating, and the DTC and freeze data will be stored.
Use of the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or removal of the 12V battery terminal will clear the D indicator light, temporary DTC, DTC, and freeze data.
